# VideoMemory

> VideoMemory is a local HTTP service for natural-language video monitors. Agents give it a camera feed and a visual condition; VideoMemory samples frames, calls a vision-language model, saves notes/evidence, and emits webhook updates when the condition happens.

Project page: https://clamepending.github.io/videomemory-site/
Source repository: https://github.com/Clamepending/videomemory
Default local server: http://127.0.0.1:5050
OpenAPI spec: http://127.0.0.1:5050/openapi.json

## What agents should do

- Use VideoMemory as the visual monitor, not as the conversational/action agent.
- Put only the visual trigger in `task_description`.
- Keep follow-up actions in the calling agent runtime.
- For "when X happens, do Y": create a VideoMemory task for "watch for X", then let the webhook receiver or channel perform "do Y".
- Prefer `POST /api/caption_frame` for one-off questions about the current frame.
- Prefer `POST /api/device/{io_id}/capture` when the user asks for a fresh photo.
- Prefer `GET /api/device/{io_id}/preview` when the user asks for the latest cached preview image.

## Run the core service

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Clamepending/videomemory
cd videomemory
uv sync
uv run videomemory-server
```

If the console script is unavailable in an older checkout, use:

```bash
uv run flask_app/app.py
```

The UI should be available at:

```text
http://127.0.0.1:5050
```

## Health and readiness

```bash
curl -fsSL http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/health
curl -fsSL http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/devices
node scripts/agent/ensure-server.mjs --json
```

Health only means the HTTP server is running. Monitors also need a reachable model runtime or configured model API key, plus camera permission for local USB or built-in cameras.

## Core HTTP flow

List devices:

```bash
curl -fsSL http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/devices
```

Ask about the current frame:

```bash
curl -fsSL -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/caption_frame \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"io_id":"0","prompt":"Describe what is visible in this camera frame."}'
```

Create a monitor:

```bash
curl -fsSL -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/tasks \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "io_id": "0",
    "task_description": "A phone is visible in the frame.",
    "bot_id": "my-agent",
    "save_note_frames": true,
    "save_note_videos": true
  }'
```

Read task notes and evidence:

```bash
curl -fsSL http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/task/0
```

Stop a monitor:

```bash
curl -fsSL -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/task/0/stop
```

## Claude Code quickstart

The published npm package exposes the one-command Claude Code path:

```bash
claude auth login
npx -y @clamepending/videomemory@0.1.9 claude
```

This starts or checks VideoMemory, wires the Claude channel webhook, opens the browser camera bridge on macOS, and launches Claude Code with VideoMemory tools enabled.

Useful prompt:

```text
Use VideoMemory to watch my FaceTime camera. Wake me when a human is visible.
```

## OpenClaw quickstart

Plugin path:

```bash
openclaw plugins install @clamepending/videomemory
```

Then in OpenClaw:

```text
/videomemory-onboard --explain
/videomemory-onboard
```

Host CLI path:

```bash
npx -y @clamepending/videomemory@0.1.9 onboard --safe --explain
npx -y @clamepending/videomemory@0.1.9 onboard --safe
```

When OpenClaw runs in Docker and VideoMemory runs on the host, use:

```text
http://host.docker.internal:5050
```

When both are in the bundled Docker network, use:

```text
http://videomemory:5050
```

## Configuration keys

Set at least one model provider key in the UI or through `PUT /api/settings/{key}`:

- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
- `VIDEO_INGESTOR_MODEL`
- `VIDEOMEMORY_OPENCLAW_WEBHOOK_URL`
- `VIDEOMEMORY_SELF_BASE_URL`

Example:

```bash
curl -fsSL -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:5050/api/settings/GOOGLE_API_KEY \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"value":"YOUR_KEY"}'
```

## Important split

Bad task description:

```text
Watch for a backpack and tell a joke in chat.
```

Better split:

```text
VideoMemory task_description: "A backpack is visible."
Agent-side action: "When the webhook arrives, tell one short backpack joke in chat."
```
